actors.text: A 95% CI for the slope of cost vs miles is (0.08, 0.14). Which interpretation is correct?
We are 95% sure the true slope is in (0.08, 0.14)
actors.reflection: Common but wrong. The 95% describes the METHOD across repeated samples, not certainty about this single interval. beta is fixed; the interval is random.

actors.text: We are 95% confident the population slope beta is between 0.08 and 0.14 dollars per mile
95% of sample slopes will fall in (0.08, 0.14)
actors.reflection: The CI describes the PARAMETER beta, not the spread of sample slopes. Individual b values vary much more widely than the CI.

completion.rule: any correct QuestionDoor switch pressed by >= 1/3 of online players, then any Player walks onto Goal
